chapter: Chapter: How the State Government Works (Social and Political Life-II)

questions.question: What is the full form of MLA?
questions.explanation: MLA stands for Member of Legislative Assembly. They are the representatives elected by the people.

questions.question: Who elects the Members of the Legislative Assembly (MLAs)?
questions.explanation: Every MLA is elected by the people living in their specific constituency.

questions.question: The state of Himachal Pradesh is divided into how many assembly constituencies?
questions.explanation: As per the NCERT textbook example, Himachal Pradesh is divided into 68 assembly constituencies.

questions.question: A political party whose MLAs have won more than half the number of constituencies in a state is said to be in a ______.
questions.explanation: In a democracy, a party with more than half the seats is considered to have the majority and is invited to form the government.

questions.question: The political party that has the majority is called the ______ party.
questions.explanation: The party that wins the majority of seats and forms the government is called the ruling party.

questions.question: All the other members who are not part of the ruling party are called the ______.
questions.explanation: The elected members who are not part of the ruling party form the opposition in the Legislative Assembly.

questions.question: Who is the head of the State in India?
questions.explanation: The Governor is the head of the State. He/She is appointed by the Central Government.

questions.question: Who appoints the Chief Minister and other ministers?
questions.explanation: After the elections, the Governor appoints the leader of the majority party as the Chief Minister.

questions.question: The place where all the MLAs, from both the ruling and opposition parties, meet to discuss things is called the ______.
questions.explanation: The Legislative Assembly is the venue where MLAs gather to debate, discuss, and pass laws.

questions.question: Who has the main responsibility of running various government departments or ministries?

options.B: The Chief Minister and other ministers

questions.explanation: The Chief Minister and their cabinet of ministers are responsible for running the government departments.

questions.question: In a Legislative Assembly, MLAs can express their opinions and ask ______.
questions.explanation: MLAs have the right to ask questions to the ministers regarding the work of their departments.

questions.question: What is the common way for the government to explain its work to the public after a discussion in the assembly?

options.B: By sending letters to every house

questions.explanation: Ministers often hold press conferences to inform journalists and the public about the decisions taken by the government.

questions.question: Laws for the entire state are made in the ______.
questions.explanation: The Legislative Assembly of each state is the body that makes laws for that particular state.

questions.question: The government works through various departments like Agriculture, Health, and ______.
questions.explanation: The Education Department is one of the many departments through which the state government functions.

questions.question: What happened in the Patalpuram district according to the story in the book?

options.A: There was a shortage of water and people were falling ill

questions.explanation: The chapter starts with a story about a water crisis and diarrhea outbreak in Patalpuram.

questions.question: Who is responsible for answering the questions asked in the Legislative Assembly?

options.B: The ministers of the ruling party

questions.explanation: The ministers are responsible for responding to questions regarding the functioning of their respective departments.

questions.question: A 'constituency' is a particular area from which all the ______ living there choose their representatives.
questions.explanation: A constituency is a geographical area where voters elect a representative to the Legislative Assembly.

questions.question: When two or more parties come together to form a government, it is called a ______ government.
questions.explanation: A coalition government is formed when no single party gets a clear majority.

questions.question: The Governor is appointed by the ______ Government.
questions.explanation: The Central Government appoints the Governor to ensure that the State Government works within the rules of the Constitution.

questions.question: Besides the Legislative Assembly, what is another way people express their views about the government?
questions.explanation: Media plays a vital role in discussing government work and conveying public opinion.

questions.question: Every state in India has a ______.
questions.explanation: All Indian states have a Legislative Assembly (Vidhan Sabha) to make laws at the state level.

questions.question: The Chief Minister is the head of the ______.
questions.explanation: While the Governor is the formal head, the Chief Minister is the actual head of the state government.

questions.question: Who can organize a 'Morcha' or protest to demand action from the government?
questions.explanation: Protests and Morchas are democratic ways for the public and opposition to show their disagreement with government policies.

questions.question: An MLA who is also a minister has ______ responsibilities.
questions.explanation: An MLA-minister must look after their specific constituency and also handle their assigned government department.

questions.question: The word 'Government' usually refers to the ______.

options.B: All the buildings in the city

questions.explanation: In common usage, 'government' refers to the executive branch, which includes the ministers and the departments they head.
